notes: Sentence-final 'loh' on a value judgement ('enak banget' = 'really delicious') functions as gentle insistence — the speaker wants the listener to register the strength of their opinion. English 'you know' lands the same conversational register. Validates that the loh rule fires equally on opinion-statement utterances, not just emotional ones (case_001). VERIFY WITH GIRLFRIEND: confirm 'you know' reads naturally in this register — alternative 'really' or 'I'm telling you' might be more native depending on dialect.
